content.boundary: Applies only to the admissions mechanism (exam design, scoring thresholds, ranking algorithm inputs) for 6- and 8-year gimnázium entry; does not touch track existence, capacity, or the statutory age of first selection
content.change_lever: Redesign the centrally administered entrance exam and admissions algorithm to reduce coachability and add SES-aware weighting or proxy adjustments, while keeping track lengths, legal structure, and free school choice untouched
content.name: Coaching-resistant, SES-weighted admissions reform within the existing track structure
content.system_problem: Entry to 6/8-year gimnázium tracks functions as a capital-intensive competition: parental investment in coaching (3.5 hrs/week, 34,000 HUF/month) and pre-existing tertiary-educated parental background overwhelmingly predict admission, producing near-total exclusion of disadvantaged children before any ability signal is meaningfully tested
